headline: Agentic coding loops moved to the center this quarter as engineers shifted from manual prompting to orchestrated agent-on-agent systems.
body: The clearest rotation is toward autonomous agent orchestration: the builders behind Claude Code and OpenClaw describe replacing hand-written prompts with continuous loops where an orchestrator spawns parallel worker agents with database observability [11], and Z AI shipped Z Code, a Codex-like coding agent with a free 5 million token daily tier for GLM-5.2 [0]. Supporting this, AI-assisted development, system architecture, and prompt caching with cache hit rate all registered as top movers, while tool calling weakened. A cluster of web-sandboxing techniques broke out as new entrants, including double iframe, CSP, sandbox, and srcdoc, alongside privacy work in encrypted reasoning and privacy-preserving AI. On the infrastructure and talent side, an x86 AI Compute Extensions specification appeared for CPU-level acceleration [5], and Noam Shazeer joined OpenAI [8][9].

cited_items.title: FULLY FREE GLM-5.2 + Z-Code: This is ACTUALLY GOOD!
The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
